justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with 3 independent data planes where no raw PII crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA compliance addressed architecturally via regional sharding and PII-stripping ACL on the Global Event Bridge. Global dashboard reads only anonymized/aggregated metrics projections, never cross-region raw data. Courier assignment regional isolation enforced by construction — cross-region assignment is physically impossible since each region's data plane has no visibility into other regions' courier IDs. Trade-offs explicitly call out 3× infrastructure operational overhead, deployment consistency, and inability to do cross-region queries. Degraded EU service designed with regional caches serving stale data while blocking new order creation.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation model with clear data flow diagrams showing PII never leaves its region
Global Event Bridge as Anti-Corruption Layer with automated PII schema validation is a strong architectural enforcement mechanism
Courier-region binding described as physically enforced by data boundary, not just policy — exactly the right framing
Global dashboard design is well-specified: pre-aggregated materialized views, no fan-out queries to regions at read time
Degraded mode clearly scoped: view existing orders from cache, cannot place new orders — matches the 'degraded but functional' requirement
Risks section covers schema migration across regions, stale dashboard during outage, and operational complexity
Practical implementation guidance with phased rollout and explicit pitfalls to avoid
Customer relocation workflow (EU→US data migration) addressed as a risk with mitigation — good foresight

CCPA is mentioned alongside GDPR but not explicitly distinguished — the architecture handles both via the same regional isolation, but specific CCPA requirements (right to delete, opt-out of sale) are not called out separately
Region Registry as a globally-replicated store could itself become a compliance discussion point — even though it stores only region mappings, the replication strategy deserves more scrutiny

agent_output_excerpt:
## PR Review — MJ (Architecture)

---

### Executive Summary

The logistics platform requires a **regionally-sovereign, federated architecture** where each region (EU, US, APAC) operates as an autonomous unit owning its customer and courier data, while a global coordination layer aggregates operational metrics via event streaming. Data residency is enforced architecturally — not by policy — through regional data planes that never replicate PII cross-border, with a thin global control plane that
